tweets.full_text:
Three days ago, GPT-5.6 deleted my Mac’s home directory.

It absolutely sucked.

But so many OpenAI folks reached out, and @gdb called me and offered to do anything he could to help.

Massive props to OpenAI for handling a shitty situation incredibly well.
tweets.summary: GPT-5.6三天前删除用户Mac主目录，但OpenAI团队积极响应并提供帮助，展现良好客户服务。
angles: GPT-5.6险些摧毁用户电脑：AI安全警钟
OpenAI如何化解危机：从灾难到信任重建
AI工具的黑天鹅事件与企业责任

tweets.full_text:
We’ve built a new way to ship software: Agent Teams in Antigravity.

Just run /teamwork-preview to spin up a dynamic team of specialized subagents. They coordinate in the background to plan, build, and verify complex engineering tasks in parallel. https://t.co/KKQc6JazhP
tweets.summary: Antigravity推出Agent Teams功能，支持动态团队协作完成复杂工程任务。通过/teamwork-preview命令启动，多个专业子代理并行规划、构建、验证。
angles: AI代理团队首次实现并行工程任务协作

So what about all those people who https://t.co/Y89w3bZIlb

tweets.full_text:
actual answer: because employees are swapped between openai and anthropic so often that at this point it is basically one company in two different silos

anything known in one company gets implemented in the other very quickly

google, spacexai, and meta do not have this
tweets.summary: 观点：OpenAI与Anthropic员工频繁流动，两家公司实质上成为不同筒仓内的一体，技术快速互相实现；Google、SpaceX AI、Meta则无此现象。
angles: OpenAI vs Anthropic：为什么他们其实是一家公司？

tweets.full_text: The biggest mistake young founders make is a variant of this: to build something you imagine people want, instead of studying them and figuring out what they actually do want. But the hack for beating this is to roll with your solipsism and make something for yourself.
tweets.summary: Paul Graham分享创业建议：创始人应研究用户真实需求而非想象需求，建议自己使用产品。
angles: Paul Graham揭秘：创始人最常犯的致命错误
如何避免想象用户需求的陷阱
自用产品法则：创业成功的隐藏密码

The backstory makes

tweets.full_text: An initial startup idea can't usually be both grand and precise. In practice they're usually either grand and vague or precise and small. Precise and small is better. You know who your initial users are, and you expand outward. With grand and vague you can't even get started.
tweets.summary: Paul Graham观点：初创公司早期想法难以同时宏大且精准，应选择精准且小众的方向，明确初始用户后逐步扩展。
